threat_classes.title: Malicious Repo Content
primary_defenses: Normalize repository paths and command roots before execution.
Deny path escapes and hidden host roots.
Run repository code only through ToolExecutor policy.
residual_risks: Valid-looking source or test code can still manipulate agents semantically.

threat_classes.title: Malicious Tool Output
primary_defenses: Label tool output as untrusted context.
Bind tool output to artifact digests.
Reject malformed output schemas.
residual_risks: Schema-valid output can still contain misleading prose or partial evidence.

threat_classes.title: Policy Evasion
primary_defenses: Normalize command specs before policy evaluation.
Apply explicit allowlists and denylists.
Require environment, network, and credential lease checks.
residual_risks: A stale policy profile can omit a newly risky command form.

threat_classes.title: Test Weakening
primary_defenses: Compare planned acceptance checks with executed test evidence.
Record gate findings for skipped, removed, or weakened tests.
Require explicit N/A rationale for omitted verification.
residual_risks: Semantic weakening may require reviewer and acceptance calibration evidence.

threat_classes.title: Secret Exposure
primary_defenses: Issue credentials only through CredentialLease records.
Redact before public projection.
Keep raw sensitive artifacts quarantined behind private paths.
residual_risks: A tool can print secret-shaped data before redaction classifies it.

threat_classes.title: Supply Chain Drift
primary_defenses: Record ToolchainProfile data.
Compare dependency manifests across clean environments.
Fail the gate when runtime versions drift from the RunSpec.
residual_risks: Compatible dependency ranges can still admit behavior changes.

threat_classes.title: Artifact Tampering
primary_defenses: Store evidence under content-addressed digests.
Verify blob digests before report projection.
Compute a stable RunBundle root digest for replay.
residual_risks: Deleted local artifact bytes cannot be reconstructed by Conveyor.

threat_classes.title: Reviewer Rubber Stamps
primary_defenses: Separate reviewer profiles from implementer profiles.
Bind reviews to dossier digests and rubric versions.
Reject malformed or stale reviews before gate use.
residual_risks: A review can be shallow but schema-valid unless checks require evidence.

threat_classes.title: Gate False Negatives
primary_defenses: Make gate decisions deterministic.
Fail closed on stale, missing, malformed, or inconsistent evidence.
Require blocking findings to include severity, category, artifact refs, and NextAction.
residual_risks: The gate can only evaluate checks represented in current policy and schema.

threat_classes.title: Internal DB Probing
primary_defenses: Access internal state through scoped resources and append-only ledger APIs.
Project public reports only from approved artifact records.
Redact private run state before report projection.
residual_risks: Authorization bugs can expose internal state before projection filters apply.

threat_classes.title: Host Escape And Overreach
primary_defenses: Restrict Docker capabilities, filesystem mounts, host networking, and process privileges.
Reject commands outside declared roots or network policy before execution.
Make unsafe host prerequisites explicit in doctor checks.
residual_risks: Kernel, container runtime, or host misconfiguration can exceed Conveyor control.
